message:
chore(deps): Add diff-match-patch types and update TypeScript config

DEPENDENCIES (package.json, bun.lock):
- Add @types/diff-match-patch ^1.0.36 to devDependencies
  Required for type safety when using jsondiffpatch library which
  depends on diff-match-patch internally

TYPESCRIPT CONFIG (tsconfig.json):
- Change module from "esnext" to "Node16"
  Better alignment with Node.js module resolution in ESM context
- Change moduleResolution from "bundler" to "Node16"
  More accurate resolution for .js extensions in import paths
- Remove explicit typeRoots and types configuration
  Let TypeScript use defaults, simplifying configuration
- Add types.d.ts to include array
  Enables project-specific type declarations
- Remove node_modules from exclude array
  Not needed when using standard resolution

WHY:
The Node16 module/resolution settings better match our ESM-first approach
with explicit .js extensions in imports. The diff-match-patch types prevent
'any' warnings when working with the jsondiffpatch dependency.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a3y4","title":"[Semantic] EmbeddingCache type - Cached embeddings storage","description":"Cache structure for storing pre-computed embeddings.

File: ~/.cass-memory/embeddings/bullets.json

Structure:
```typescript
interface EmbeddingCache {
  version: string;           // Cache format version
  model: string;             // Model used (for invalidation)
  bullets: Record<string, {
    contentHash: string;     // Hash of content when computed
    embedding: number[];     // 384-dimensional vector
    computedAt: string;      // ISO timestamp
  }>;
}
```

Invalidation logic:
- If content hash changed → recompute
- If model changed → recompute all
- If cache version changed → rebuild cache

Cache benefits:
- Embeddings computed once
- Startup time: ~50ms vs ~300ms
- Model stays loaded in memory

Cache size:
- 100 bullets × 384 dims × 8 bytes = ~300KB
- Negligible disk space
- Fast to load/save

Example:
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "model": "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
  "bullets": {
    "b-abc123": {
      "contentHash": "sha256:...",
      "embedding": [0.123, -0.456, ...],
      "computedAt": "2025-12-07T10:00:00Z"
    }
  }
}
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:32.970005-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:56.932408-05:00","comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-a3y4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cache structure for storing pre-computed embeddings.

Invalidation logic:

Cache benefits:

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a4g","title":"[Utils] formatRelativeTime - Human-readable relative time","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:11.043941-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:11.043941-05:00","comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-a4g","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function formatRelativeTime(isoDate: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Parse isoDate to Date object
- Calculate difference from now in milliseconds
- Convert to days: Math.floor(diff / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24))
- Return string based on days:
  - 0 days: "today"
  - 1 day: "yesterday"
  - 2-6 days: "N days ago"
  - 7-29 days: "N weeks ago" (divide by 7, round)
  - 30+ days: "N months ago" (divide by 30, round)

## Why This Matters
Used in human-readable output (not JSON mode). Makes timestamps easier to understand at a glance. "2 days ago" is clearer than "2024-01-13T14:30:00.000Z".

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Future dates - return "in the future" or handle as 0 days
- Invalid ISO string - throw error
- Very old dates - "N months ago" can get large, consider "N years ago" for 365+ days","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-alcn","title":"[CLI:reflect] updateProcessedLog - Track processed sessions","description":"Record processed sessions to prevent reprocessing.

Function signature:
```typescript
async function updateProcessedLog(
  log: ProcessedLog,
  entries: ProcessedEntry[]
): Promise<void>
```

Implementation:
1. Load existing processed.log:
   - File location: ${memoryDir}/processed.log
   - Tab-delimited format
   - Create if doesn't exist

2. Add new entries:
   - Each entry represents one processed session
   - Format: {id}\t{timestamp}\t{sessionPath}\t{deltasProposed}\t{deltasApplied}\n
   - Example: sess-abc123\t2025-01-15T10:30:00Z\t/Users/x/.claude/sessions/001.jsonl\t5\t3

3. Write to disk:
   - Append mode (don't overwrite)
   - Atomic write (temp file + rename)
   - Ensure proper permissions

ProcessedEntry type:
```typescript
interface ProcessedEntry {
  id: string;              // session ID
  timestamp: string;       // ISO 8601
  sessionPath: string;     // absolute path
  deltasProposed: number;  // from reflector
  deltasApplied: number;   // from curator
}
```

ProcessedLog class:
```typescript
class ProcessedLog {
  private entries: Map<string, ProcessedEntry>;
  
  load(): Promise<void>
  has(sessionPath: string): boolean
  add(entry: ProcessedEntry): void
  save(): Promise<void>
}
```

The log prevents duplicate processing and provides audit trail.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:18.282321-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:18.282321-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-ank","title":"[Diary] loadDiaryEntry - Read from disk","description":"Read and validate diary entry from disk.

Function Signature:
async function loadDiaryEntry(diaryPath: string): Promise<DiaryEntry>

Process:
1. Verify file exists at diaryPath
2. Read file contents as UTF-8 string
3. Parse JSON string to object
4. Validate against DiaryEntrySchema using Zod
5. Return validated DiaryEntry

Validation Features:
- Type checking for all fields
- Required field validation
- Date format validation for timestamp
- Array content validation
- Status enum validation (success/failure/mixed)

Error Handling:
- FileNotFoundError if path doesn't exist
- JSONParseError if invalid JSON
- SchemaValidationError if missing/invalid fields
- Detailed error messages for debugging

Schema Migration:
- If old diary version detected, apply migration
- Add default values for new optional fields
- Preserve backward compatibility

Key Features:
- Full schema validation ensures type safety
- Detailed validation errors for debugging
- Migration support for schema evolution
- UTF-8 encoding handling

Use Cases:
- Loading diary for evidence correlation
- Reading diaries for stats computation
- Fetching diary for display in UI
- Validating diary integrity

Output:
Returns fully validated DiaryEntry object ready for use.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.388329-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:18.411032-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-apt","title":"[Cass] cassSearch - Execute cass search with options","description":"Implement cassSearch() to execute cass search with comprehensive options.

## Signature
```typescript
interface CassSearchOptions {
  limit?: number;
  days?: number;
  agent?: string;
  workspace?: string;
  fields?: string;
  maxTokens?: number;
  highlight?: boolean;
  timeout?: number;
}

interface CassHit {
  sessionPath: string;
  lineNumber: number;
  content: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  score?: number;
}

async function cassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## CLI Command
`cass search "{query}" --robot [--limit N] [--days N] [--agent A] [--workspace W] [--fields F] [--max-tokens N] [--highlight] [--timeout N]`

## Implementation
- Build command with all provided options
- Use execFile() with maxBuffer: 50MB for large results
- Parse JSON output from --robot flag
- Return array of CassHit objects

## Error Handling
Check exit code and handle intelligently:
- Exit 0: Success, parse and return hits
- Exit 3: Index missing - rebuild index, then retry once
- Exit 10: Timeout - reduce limit by 50%, retry once
- Exit 4: Not found - return empty array
- Other errors: Log and return empty array

## Options Details
- limit: Max hits to return (default: 10)
- days: Only search last N days
- agent: Filter by agent name (e.g., "claude-code")
- workspace: Filter by workspace path
- fields: Comma-separated fields to search
- maxTokens: Truncate results to N tokens
- highlight: Include highlighting markers in results
- timeout: Command timeout in seconds

## Usage
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix", { 
  limit: 20, 
  days: 7, 
  agent: "claude-code" 
});
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.153732-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:10.078585-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:00.15937-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ar2","title":"[Curator] invertToAntiPattern - Convert harmful rule to pitfall","description":"Convert a harmful bullet into an anti-pattern (learning from mistakes).

Signature:
function invertToAntiPattern(bullet: PlaybookBullet): PlaybookBullet

KEY INNOVATION: Instead of deleting harmful rules, INVERT them to preserve learning.

Process:
1. Create new bullet with inverted semantics:
   - content: Prefix with 'AVOID: ' or 'DON\'T: '
   - Example: 'Use any type' → 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason'
   
2. Set bullet fields:
   - type: 'anti-pattern'
   - kind: 'anti_pattern'
   - category: same as original
   - maturity: 'candidate' (starts fresh)
   - state: 'active'
   
3. Preserve provenance:
   - source: copy from original
   - originalId: link back to harmful bullet
   - createdAt: current timestamp
   - derivedFrom: original bullet ID
   
4. Reset feedback (fresh start):
   - helpfulCount: 0
   - harmfulCount: 0
   - helpfulEvents: []
   - harmfulEvents: []
   
5. Return new anti-pattern bullet

Why inversion matters:
- Preserves WHAT NOT TO DO
- Prevents future developers from making same mistake
- Converts negative experience into positive guidance
- Maintains complete learning history

Example transformation:
Original (harmful): 'Always use any type for flexibility'
Anti-pattern: 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason. Prefer specific types for type safety.'

The anti-pattern must earn trust like any other candidate bullet.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.707658-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:47.213445-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b5q","title":"[Security] compileExtraPatterns - User pattern compilation","description":"Implement compileExtraPatterns() to convert user pattern strings to RegExp objects.

Function signature:
```typescript
function compileExtraPatterns(patterns: string[]): RegExp[]
```

Implementation requirements:
1. Parse string patterns into RegExp objects
2. Add 'gi' flags automatically (global, case-insensitive)
3. Catch and warn on invalid regex syntax
4. Return only valid patterns (skip invalid ones)

Error handling:
- Log warning for invalid patterns: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: <pattern>"
- Continue processing remaining patterns
- Never throw - return partial results

Example:
```typescript
const patterns = [
  "INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY",
  "[invalid(regex",  // syntax error
  "MY_SECRET_\\w+"
];
const compiled = compileExtraPatterns(patterns);
// Returns: [/INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY/gi, /MY_SECRET_\w+/gi]
// Logs: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: [invalid(regex"
```

Called at startup to pre-compile user patterns from config.

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Implemented compileExtraPatterns in src/sanitize.ts with gi flags, error handling, and warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:51.253402-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.249516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:52:23.874542-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ixln","title":"[CLI:init] createDirectoryStructure - Set up directories","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:32.132292-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:32.132292-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-j2i","title":"[Curator] handleReplaceDelta - Update bullet content","description":"Update bullet content while preserving feedback history.

Signature:
function handleReplaceDelta(
  playbook: Playbook,
  delta: ReplaceDelta
): { applied: boolean }

Process:
1. Find bullet by ID
   - If not found: return applied: false
2. Update bullet.content to delta.newContent
3. Update bullet.updatedAt to current timestamp
4. DO NOT reset helpfulCount or harmfulCount
5. DO NOT clear helpfulEvents or harmfulEvents
6. DO NOT reset maturity state
7. Return applied: true

KEY PRINCIPLE: Feedback history is PRESERVED across content updates.

Why preserve history?
- The rule's value/harm is tied to its semantic meaning, not exact wording
- Allows refining phrasing without losing trust
- Prevents gaming the system by rewording harmful rules

Example use case:
- Original: 'Use any type for flexibility'
- Gets harmful feedback
- Replace: 'Use any type when prototyping'
- Still carries harmful history - must earn back trust

This maintains accountability while allowing improvement.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:59.97769-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:47.9519-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-jhuj","title":"[CLI:audit] scanSessionsForViolations - Find rule violations","description":"Core logic to scan sessions and identify playbook rule violations.

Function signature:
```typescript
async function scanSessionsForViolations(
  sessions: string[],
  playbook: Playbook,
  config: Config
): Promise<AuditViolation[]>
```

Implementation:
1. For each session path:
   - Load or export session content
   - Extract key events: commits, file changes, commands
   
2. For each active playbook bullet:
   - Prepare violation check prompt:
     ```
     Session summary: {sessionSummary}
     Rule: {bullet.content}
     Category: {bullet.category}
     
     Did this session violate this rule? If yes, provide evidence.
     ```
   
   - Call LLM (fast model)
   - Parse response for violation detection
   
3. Record violations:
   ```typescript
   interface AuditViolation {
     bulletId: string;
     bulletContent: string;
     sessionPath: string;
     timestamp: string;
     severity: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
     evidence: string;
   }
   ```
   
4. Severity mapping:
   - proven → high
   - accepted → medium
   - candidate → low

Optimizations:
- Batch LLM calls where possible
- Cache session summaries
- Skip deprecated bullets
- Early exit if no active rules

Edge cases:
- Handle session load failures gracefully
- Skip sessions with no meaningful content
- Handle LLM parsing errors (assume no violation)

Return array of all violations found across all sessions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:11.808386-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:11.808386-05:00"}

  metadata: { sessionPath: string; agent: string; workspace?: string },
  config: Config

Implementation:
1. Build prompt from PROMPTS.diary template
2. Substitute variables: sessionPath, agent, workspace, content
3. Truncate sessionContent if >50000 chars (to avoid token limits)
4. Call generateObject with:
   - schema: provided Zod schema
   - prompt: built prompt
   - temperature: 0.3 (low for consistency)
   - model: from config
5. Return typed object (Zod validates structure)

Used by: digest command to extract structured insights from agent sessions.

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-k0qp","title":"[Semantic] saveEmbeddingCache - Persist cache to disk","description":"Persist embedding cache to disk atomically.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function saveEmbeddingCache(cache: EmbeddingCache): Promise<void>
```

Process:
1. Serialize cache to JSON
2. Write to temp file (~/.cass-memory/embeddings/bullets.json.tmp)
3. Atomic move to final location
4. Handle errors gracefully

Atomicity:
- Write to temp file first
- Move is atomic on most filesystems
- Prevents corruption if crash during write

Error handling:
- Directory not exist → create
- Permission denied → log error
- Disk full → log error, keep old cache
- JSON serialization error → log and skip

File size:
- 100 bullets: ~300KB
- 1000 bullets: ~3MB
- Gzip compression could reduce 50% (future)

When called:
- After loadOrComputeEmbeddings
- After batch embedding updates
- Before process exit (cleanup)

Example:
```typescript
await saveEmbeddingCache({
  version: "1.0",
  model: "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
  bullets: {
    "b-123": { contentHash: "...", embedding: [...], computedAt: "..." }
  }
});
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:44.691385-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:56.690349-05:00","comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-k0qp","author":"jemanuel","text":"Persist embedding cache to disk atomically.

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-k2k","title":"[Reflector] ReflectionStats - Track reflection effectiveness","description":"interface ReflectionStats {
  sessionPath: string;
  iterations: number;
  deltasPerIteration: number[];
  totalDeltas: number;
  deltasByType: Record<string, number>;
  duration: number;
}

Track for analysis of reflection quality

PURPOSE:
- Monitor reflection effectiveness
- Identify when early exit is working
- Debug reflection issues
- Optimize maxReflectorIterations

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
```json
{
  "sessionPath": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
  "iterations": 3,
  "deltasPerIteration": [12, 5, 0],
  "totalDeltas": 17,
  "deltasByType": {
    "add": 10,
    "helpful": 4,
    "harmful": 2,
    "replace": 1,
    "deprecate": 0
  },
  "duration": 4523
}
```

ANALYSIS INSIGHTS:
- If iterations = 1 always: prompt may be too narrow
- If deltasPerIteration decreases: good (finding less each time)
- If duration > 10s: consider reducing maxReflectorIterations
- If totalDeltas = 0: diary may be empty or LLM issue

COLLECTION POINTS:
- Start: record sessionPath, start time
- Each iteration: push deltas count
- End: record total, duration, type breakdown

STORAGE:
- Log to console in verbose mode
- Optional: append to .cass/reflection-stats.jsonl

Used in reflectOnSession()","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:02.213483-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:06:29.959739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:05:24.166253-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-kam","title":"[Curator] handleDeprecateDelta - Mark bullet as deprecated","description":"Explicitly mark a bullet as deprecated with optional replacement.

Signature:
function handleDeprecateDelta(
  playbook: Playbook,
  delta: DeprecateDelta
): { applied: boolean }

Process:
1. Find bullet by ID
   - If not found: return applied: false
2. Update bullet fields:
   - state: 'retired'
   - maturity: 'deprecated'
   - deprecationReason: delta.reason (required)
   - replacedBy: delta.replacedBy (optional bullet ID)
   - updatedAt: current timestamp
3. Return applied: true

When is this used?
- Manual deprecation by user (cm forget)
- Merging bullets (old bullets point to merged result)
- Replacing outdated rules with better versions

Important notes:
- Deprecated bullets are NOT deleted
- They're moved to playbook.deprecated array during pruning
- History is preserved for audit trail
- replacedBy provides migration path

Example deprecation chain:
1. Bullet A is deprecated with replacedBy: B
2. Bullet B later deprecated with replacedBy: C
3. System can trace evolution: A → B → C

This maintains continuity while allowing playbook evolution.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.887743-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:18:09.465522-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xw0","title":"[Utils] generateDiaryId - Unique diary entry identifier","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.048552-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.048552-05:00","comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-xw0","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function generateDiaryId(sessionPath: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Format: "diary-{hash}"
- Combine sessionPath + Date.now() into single string
- Hash using hashContent() (which uses SHA-256)
- Prefix with "diary-" for clarity
- Deterministic for same session at same millisecond

## Why This Matters
Diary entries need unique IDs that are tied to session but not globally unique. This allows same session to have multiple diary entries if processed at different times, while preventing exact duplicates.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Same session processed twice in same ms - same ID (prevents duplicate diary entries)
- Same session processed at different times - different IDs
- Different sessions - different IDs
- Empty sessionPath - still generates ID based on timestamp","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y22j","title":"[Docs] Configuration reference documentation","description":"Document all configuration options with types, defaults, and examples.

Configuration sources (precedence order):
1. Command-line flags (highest)
2. Environment variables
3. Repo config (.cass/config.yaml)
4. Global config (~/.cass-memory/config.json)
5. Defaults (lowest)

Global config: llm_provider, default_model, confidence_threshold, decay_half_life_days, max_bullets_per_section
Repo config: project_name, playbook_path, diary_path, session_path, validation_rules
Environment variables: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CASS_MEMORY_CONFIG, CASS_MEMORY_DEBUG

Include JSON schema for validation and example configs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:28.411185-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:13.944052-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y5s","title":"[Curator] pruneDeprecated - Remove deprecated from active list","description":"Remove deprecated bullets from active list and move to deprecated section.

Signature:
function pruneDeprecated(playbook: Playbook): number

Process:
1. Initialize pruned counter
2. Filter playbook.bullets:
   - Keep bullets where maturity !== 'deprecated'
   - Collect deprecated bullets
3. For each deprecated bullet:
   - Add to playbook.deprecated array (if it exists)
   - Increment pruned counter
4. Update playbook.bullets to filtered array
5. Return pruned count

Important notes:
- Does NOT delete data
- Deprecated bullets move to separate section for history
- Maintains complete audit trail
- Allows future analysis of what didn't work

Why separate active from deprecated?
- Keeps active playbook focused and clean
- Prevents deprecated rules from being used
- Preserves history for learning
- Enables deprecation analytics

Playbook structure after pruning:
{
  bullets: [...active bullets...],
  deprecated: [...deprecated bullets...],
  stats: {
    totalActive: bullets.length,
    totalDeprecated: deprecated.length
  }
}

This is called at the END of curatePlaybook, after auto-inversion.

Prune order matters:
1. Process all deltas
2. Auto-invert harmful bullets (creates deprecations)
3. Prune deprecated (moves them out)
4. Return final playbook","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:06.4804-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:46.957599-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-y6t","title":"[Types] CommandResult and Error Types","description":"## Overview
Define command result and error types for consistent CLI output and error handling across all cass-memory commands.

## Purpose
Standardize:
1. Success/failure responses from all commands
2. Error reporting with actionable messages
3. Data payloads for JSON output mode
4. Audit violation reporting
5. Health check results

## CommandResult

Standard return type for all CLI commands.

Fields:

success: boolean
- True if command completed successfully
- False if command failed
- Example: true

message: string
- Human-readable summary of result
- Success: 'Added 3 new rules to playbook'
- Failure: 'Failed to load playbook: file not found'
- Required: Always present

data: unknown | undefined
- Optional command-specific payload
- Type varies by command
- Examples:
  - context command: ContextResult
  - stats command: StatsReport
  - validate command: ValidationReport
- JSON mode: Serialized to output

error: string | undefined
- Optional error details for failures
- Stack trace (in verbose mode)
- Technical details
- Example: 'ENOENT: no such file or directory, open ~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml'

## AuditViolation

Individual violation found by audit command.

Fields:

bulletId: string
- ID of bullet that was violated or contradicted
- Example: 'b-abc123'

bulletContent: string
- Text of the rule that was violated
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator for JWT validation'

sessionPath: string
- Path to session where violation occurred
- Example: '/cass/2025-12-07-api-impl.jsonl'

evidence: string
- Excerpt showing the violation
- Example: 'Used direct JWT decode without TokenValidator'

severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
- How serious the violation is
- low: Stylistic deviation
- medium: Best practice not followed
- high: Security/correctness issue

## AuditResult

Complete audit report from audit command.

Fields:

sessionsScanned: number
- Count of sessions analyzed
- Example: 25

violations: AuditViolation[]
- Array of found violations
- Grouped by: severity, then bulletId
- Example: [{ bulletId: 'b-abc', bulletContent: 'Use TokenValidator', sessionPath: '/cass/...', evidence: '...', severity: 'high' }]

suggestions: string[]
- Actionable recommendations
- Examples:
  - 'Consider deprecating rule b-xyz - violated in 80% of recent sessions'
  - 'Rule b-abc is consistently followed - promote to proven'
  - 'Add anti-pattern for direct JWT decode based on 5 violations'

## ErrorTypes

Common error categories for better error handling.

Categories:

ConfigError:
- Config file not found
- Invalid config format
- Missing required config fields

PlaybookError:
- Playbook file not found
- Invalid playbook YAML
- Schema validation failure

CassError:
- cass executable not found
- cass search failed
- cass timeout

ValidationError:
- Invalid bullet data
- Rule validation failed
- Evidence gate not passed

LLMError:
- API key missing
- API call failed
- Rate limit exceeded
- Invalid response format

## Success Response Examples

Context command:
{ success: true, message: 'Found 12 relevant rules for task', data: { task: '...', relevantBullets: [...], ... } }

Reflect command:
{ success: true, message: 'Processed 5 sessions, added 3 rules, updated 2', data: { addedCount: 3, updatedCount: 2 } }

Stats command:
{ success: true, message: 'Playbook statistics', data: { totalBullets: 42, activeCount: 35, ... } }

## Error Response Examples

Config error:
{ success: false, message: 'Config file not found', error: 'ENOENT: ~/.cass-memory/config.yaml' }

Playbook error:
{ success: false, message: 'Invalid playbook format', error: 'YAML parsing failed at line 42: unexpected indent' }

Validation error:
{ success: false, message: 'Validation failed', error: 'Insufficient evidence: only 1 session found' }

## JSON Output Mode

When config.jsonOutput = true:
- All commands return CommandResult as JSON
- Suppress human-friendly formatting
- Include full data payload
- Errors as structured objects

Example:
{ "success": true, "message": "Context retrieved", "data": { "task": "...", "relevantBullets": [...] } }

## Acceptance Criteria
- CommandResult interface with all fields
- AuditViolation interface
- AuditResult interface
- Error type definitions
- Success/error response examples
- JSON serialization support
- Zod schemas for validation
- TypeScript exports
- JSDoc documentation
- Consistent error messages across commands","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:08.437115-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:26.871514-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y7x","title":"[Generator] separateAntiPatterns - Split rules from pitfalls","description":"Separate anti-patterns from regular rules for distinct presentation.

SIGNATURE:
function separateAntiPatterns(scoredBullets: ScoredBullet[]): { rules: ScoredBullet[]; antiPatterns: ScoredBullet[] }

PURPOSE:
Split scored bullets into two categories for different sections of the context output. Anti-patterns (pitfalls) are shown separately to highlight what NOT to do.

CLASSIFICATION LOGIC:
A bullet is an anti-pattern if:
  bullet.type === 'anti-pattern' || bullet.kind === 'anti_pattern'

Both field names supported for backwards compatibility.

OUTPUT STRUCTURE:
{
  rules: ScoredBullet[],        // Regular patterns/rules (what to do)
  antiPatterns: ScoredBullet[]  // Anti-patterns (what NOT to do)
}

PRESENTATION:
Rules appear in: 'Relevant Playbook Rules' section
Anti-patterns appear in: 'PITFALLS TO AVOID' section

EXAMPLE:
Input: [
  { id: 'b1', content: 'Use async/await for DB calls', type: 'pattern' },
  { id: 'b2', content: 'AVOID: Blocking DB calls in event loop', type: 'anti-pattern' },
  { id: 'b3', content: 'Cache frequently-read data', type: 'optimization' }
]

Output: {
  rules: [b1, b3],
  antiPatterns: [b2]
}

SCORE PRESERVATION:
Both arrays maintain the scoring metadata (relevanceScore, effectiveScore, finalScore) from scorePlaybookBullets for potential display.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Simple filter operation (no mutation)
- Preserves order within each category
- Empty antiPatterns array is valid (no pitfalls relevant)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.792421-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:20:01.027529-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:18:39.77653-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","title":"Phase 3: Advanced Features + Intelligence","description":"# EPIC: Advanced Features + Intelligence

## Purpose
Add intelligent features that dramatically improve relevance and usability:
local semantic search, multi-iteration reflection, scientific validation,
and playbook health analytics.

## Background & Reasoning
Simple keyword matching for bullet relevance achieves ~60% accuracy. By adding
local semantic embeddings via @xenova/transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 23MB model),
we can reach ~90% relevance without network calls.

The GPT Pro proposal introduced "scientific validation" - before accepting a new
rule, query cass for historical evidence. Did this pattern succeed or fail in the
past? This prevents accepting rules that contradict historical evidence.

Multi-iteration reflection (from ACE paper) runs the reflector multiple times per
session, focusing on different aspects each iteration. This catches insights that
a single pass might miss.

## Priority Features (from ROI analysis)
- P7: Local semantic search @xenova/transformers (4× impact)
- P9: Stats command playbook health dashboard (2× impact)

## Key Deliverables
1. `cass-memory validate "<rule>"` - Scientific validation against history
2. `cass-memory stats` - Playbook health dashboard
3. Local embedding-based relevance scoring
4. Multi-iteration reflector (configurable 1-3 iterations)
5. Cross-agent enrichment in diaries

## Technical Specifications
### Semantic Search
- Model: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (runs locally, ~23MB)
- Embedding dimension: 384
- Scoring: 40% keyword + 60% semantic similarity
- Cache: ~/.cass-memory/embeddings/bullets.embeddings

### Stats Dashboard Metrics
- Total bullets by scope (global/workspace)
- Distribution by maturity (candidate/established/proven/retired)
- Distribution by kind (project_convention/stack_pattern/workflow_rule/anti_pattern)
- Top performers (highest effective scores)
- At-risk bullets (negative effective scores)
- Stale bullets (no feedback in 90+ days)
- Merge candidates (high semantic similarity pairs)

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 2 completion (diary, reflect, curate pipeline)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:41.554097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:41.554097-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0ew","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.218629-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","title":"Validate Command: Scientific Validation (GPT Pro Pattern)","description":"# Feature: Validate Command (Scientific Validation)

## Purpose
Validate a proposed rule against historical evidence from cass.
This implements the GPT Pro proposal's "scientific validation" - 
treating rule proposals as hypotheses to be tested against data.

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory validate "Always use httpx instead of requests" --json
cass-memory validate "Prefer composition over inheritance" --verbose
```

## Process Flow
```
1. Extract validation keywords from proposed rule
2. Check evidence-count gate (auto-accept/reject if clear signal)
3. If ambiguous, run LLM validator with evidence
4. Return verdict with reasoning
```

## Implementation
```typescript
async function validateCommand(
  proposedRule: string,
  flags: ValidateFlags
): Promise<void> {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  
  // 1. Extract keywords
  const keywords = extractKeywords(proposedRule);
  
  // 2. Check evidence gate
  const gateResult = await evidenceCountGate(proposedRule, config);
  
  if (gateResult.action !== "needs-llm") {
    output({
      proposedRule,
      verdict: gateResult.action === "auto-accept" ? "ACCEPT" : "REJECT",
      confidence: gateResult.confidence,
      reason: `${gateResult.action}: ${gateResult.successCount} successes, ${gateResult.failureCount} failures`,
      evidence: gateResult.relevantSessions.map(h => ({
        session: h.source_path,
        snippet: h.snippet,
        outcome: classifySessionOutcome(h)
      }))
    }, formatValidationResult, flags);
    return;
  }
  
  // 3. Run LLM validator
  const validation = await runValidator(
    ValidationResultSchema,
    proposedRule,
    formatEvidenceForPrompt(gateResult.relevantSessions),
    config
  );
  
  output({
    proposedRule,
    verdict: validation.valid ? "ACCEPT" : "REJECT",
    confidence: validation.confidence || 0.7,
    reason: validation.reason,
    refinedRule: validation.refinedContent,
    evidence: gateResult.relevantSessions.map(h => ({
      session: h.source_path,
      snippet: h.snippet
    }))
  }, formatValidationResult, flags);
}
```

## Output Example
```json
{
  "proposedRule": "Always use httpx instead of requests",
  "verdict": "ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION",
  "confidence": 0.75,
  "reason": "Evidence shows httpx succeeded in 4 sessions for async use cases, but requests was fine for simple sync calls",
  "refinedRule": "Prefer httpx for async HTTP calls; requests is acceptable for simple synchronous requests",
  "evidence": [
    {
      "session": "~/.claude/sessions/abc.jsonl",
      "snippet": "Switched to httpx for async support, much cleaner code"
    },
    {
      "session": "~/.cursor/sessions/def.jsonl", 
      "snippet": "requests worked fine for this simple GET request"
    }
  ]
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:24:14.956313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:07:53.578028-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:06:51.580903-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:24:14.958057-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0ew.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.294018-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-y8o.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:25.373799-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
